content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

DATE: October 21, 1982
NO.: M-82-173

TO: Advice File Sections 82034 and 87206
FROM: Fair Political Practices Commission
        Diane Maura Fishburn
RE: Interest in a Private Pension Plan

Investments in a private pension plan must normally be disclosed. See M-79-181. However, payments received from a defined benefit pension plan qualified under Internal Revenue Code Section 401(a) are excluded from the definition of "income." Section 82030(b)(11). Accordingly, interests in this type of pension plan should not be included within the definition of "investment," and disclosure of these interests is not required.

DMF:plh
